sections.paragraph:
βδελυκτός, ή, όν (s. βδέλυγμα; Aretaeus p. 84, 21; schol. on Lucian p. 81, 25 Rabe; Syntipas p. 126, 8; Hesych.; 2 Macc 1:27; TestGad 3:2 v.l. [s. βδελύσσομαι]; Philo, Spec. Leg. 1, 323) pert. to a pers. or thing that stirs up feelings of repugnance, abhorrent, detestable.

ⓐ of pers. (w. ἀπειθής) Tit 1:16 (cp. Pr 17:15; Sir 41:5 v.l.).

ⓑ impers. (TestGad 3:2 v.l.) of divisions 1 Cl 2:6; of inordinate desires and pride 30:1.—TW.

Aretaeus Aretaeus , II a.d.—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Syntipas Syntipas , a collection of fables under the name of Syntapis; before XI a.d.—List 5

Hesych Hesych , V a.d.—List 5

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

pers. pers. = person(s)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

impers. impers. = impersonal(ly)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 172
